title: The Journey Begins
description: Stepping off the train in Kediri, feeling a mix of excitement and anxiety. The journey to Pare in a local taxi, watching the scenery shift to quiet villages. This is Kampung Inggris, where English is spoken in the streets.

title: Placement Test & Ice Breaking
description: Gathering at the camp with dozens of new faces. We introduced ourselves in broken English, laughing at our pronunciation mistakes. The warmth of the tutors put us at ease immediately.

title: The Camp Family
description: Living in a shared room with people from different islands. We sat in a circle on the floor, sharing our local snacks and telling late-night stories. In just a few days, strangers became family.

title: No Bahasa Allowed
description: The strict 'English Area' rule! Speaking Indonesian meant paying a fine of 500 rupiah per word. We got creative, using hand gestures and funny sound effects to communicate.

title: Under the Mango Tree
description: Group study sessions under the shady mango tree in the backyard. Memorizing vocabularies, practicing presentations, and drilling grammar rules together under the afternoon breeze.

title: Sweet Iced Coffee & Toast
description: Rushing to the local warung during the 15-minute class break. A glass of iced coffee for 5,000 rupiah and a warm chocolate toast was our daily fuel.

title: Afternoon Match
description: A friendly match at the local field under the golden sunset. Running in the dust, cheering for every goal, and sharing a giant bottle of cold water afterwards.

title: Late Night Debates
description: Gathering at a cozy cafe in Pare. With our laptops open, we debated philosophical topics and gossiped about camp life until the cafe closed at midnight.

title: Exploring Simpang Lima Gumul
description: Renting bicycles and riding all the way to Kediri's Arc de Triomphe look-alike, Simpang Lima Gumul. Our legs were sore, but the night lights and laughter made it worth every pedal.

title: Sheltering Under the Canopy
description: A sudden tropical downpour trapped us at a street food stall. We ate hot bakso (meatballs) while watching the rain flood the street, singing acoustic songs to the sound of thunder.

title: The Ghost Story Prank
description: Turning off all the camp lights and telling ghost stories. The boys set up a fake ghost made of a white sheet that dropped from the ceiling, causing a wave of screams and laughter that woke up the neighborhood.

title: The Farewell Speech
description: Dressed in our best clothes, standing on the stage to receive our certificates. Some cried during their farewell speeches, reflecting on how much we had grown in just two months.

title: Until We Meet Again
description: Hugging each other tight at the train station. Suitcases packed, teary eyes, and promises to meet again. We entered as strangers, left as family, and carried a piece of Pare in our hearts forever.
